action.reasoning: Observation: CUDA error 'no kernel image is available' indicates a mismatch between the installed PyTorch version and the GPU architecture. Analysis: To quickly get a baseline and measure iteration time, I will switch to CPU for training and prediction, as DLinear is very lightweight and trains fast even on CPU. I will also print the PyTorch and CUDA versions to diagnose the GPU issue for future iterations. Decision: Modify train.py and predict.py to use CPU, run the training, and submit the evaluation.
action.command: python -c "import torch; print('Torch:', torch.__version__, 'CUDA:', torch.version.cuda, 'GPU:', torch.cuda.get_device_name(0) if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'No GPU')" && python train.py
